skill.name: notion-automation
skill.description: Automate Notion pages, databases, blocks, comments, and users through Rube MCP with current tool schema lookup.
skill.summary: Guides Notion automation workflows through Rube MCP for pages, databases, blocks, comments, and users.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks around MCP tool names and parameters, not executable shell code. The hardcoded Rube MCP URL is a legitimate setup endpoint, but the skill uses a third-party MCP connection and can read or modify Notion workspace data. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to Notion Workspace
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and an active Notion OAuth connection, then instructs use of Notion tools for pages, databases, blocks, users, and comments. This is legitimate automation, but it can expose workspace content to the configured MCP service and should be used only with trusted connections.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prerequisites and setup explicitly require Rube MCP and Notion OAuth. The workflows include reading users and comments, which confirms meaningful workspace data access.

medium_findings.title: Workspace Modification and Archival Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows that can update pages, replace page content, archive pages, delete blocks, create databases, and update database schemas. These actions are expected for a Notion automation skill, but mistakes could alter or remove user-visible content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines list direct modification, archival, replace, delete, and schema update operations. The skill also notes archival behavior, which confirms the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the lines contain Markdown inline code spans and fenced examples for MCP tool names, parameters, and Notion filter syntax. I found no executable script, shell command, Ruby code, or command-injection path in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are visibly Markdown formatting around tool names and example data. There is no source file or syntax that would execute those backticks as shell commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography and System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert at line 3 and the system reconnaissance alerts on Notion tool-reference lines do not match the file context. The content is prose about Notion MCP operations, database IDs, pagination, and tool names, not cryptographic code or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive Markdown and a quick-reference table. No cryptographic algorithm, local system probing command, or environment inspection instruction is present.

content.user_title: Automate Notion Workspaces with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Manual Notion updates are slow when work spans pages, databases, comments, and content blocks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for safe Notion automation through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Finds Notion pages and databases before taking action.
Creates, updates, retrieves, archives, and duplicates Notion pages.
Queries databases, inserts rows, updates rows, and checks schemas.
Reads, appends, replaces, and archives Notion content blocks.
Lists workspace users and manages page comments through Notion tools.
Guides pagination, schema matching, and Notion filter construction.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Notion connection.
Only works with pages and databases shared with the Notion integration.
Cannot set Notion read-only fields such as formulas and rollups.
Destructive changes depend on user review and correct target IDs.

use_cases.title: Maintain Team Project Trackers
use_cases.description: Update task rows, query status views, and add project notes in shared Notion databases.

use_cases.title: Publish Structured Knowledge Pages
use_cases.description: Create pages, append sections, and refresh block content for internal documentation.

use_cases.title: Audit Workspace Content
use_cases.description: Search pages, inspect database schemas, paginate records, and collect comments for review.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Notion Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Notion automation skill to find the page named [page name]. Show the matching page titles and ask before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Database Row
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a record after checking schema.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the [database name] database, inspect its schema, then add a row with these values: [values]. Confirm any required fields first.

prompt_templates.title: Update Page Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the current blocks for [page name], summarize what will change, then append this content after approval: [content].

prompt_templates.title: Run a Workspace Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [database or page], paginate all matching records, apply this filter: [filter], update matching items with [changes], and report every modified item.

output_examples.input: Find the product roadmap database and show open projects assigned to Maya.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches for the database, checks the exact property names, applies the assignee and status filters, paginates results, and returns a concise project list.

output_examples.input: Create a meeting notes page under the weekly planning page.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the parent page, confirms the location, creates the child page, and reports the new page title and link.

output_examples.input: Replace the content on the archived onboarding page.
output_examples.output: The assistant detects the archived state, explains that content cannot be appended until the page is restored, and asks before unarchiving or changing content.

best_practices: Search for current Rube tool schemas before each workflow.
Confirm page, database, and block IDs before write actions.
Paginate reads until all Notion results are retrieved.
anti_patterns: Do not archive pages or delete blocks without explicit user approval.
Do not assume property names or field types without fetching the database schema.
Do not ignore pagination cursors when reviewing database results.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Notion API key?
faq.answer: No. It uses Rube MCP and a Notion OAuth connection managed through Rube.

faq.question: Can it edit existing Notion pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can update page properties and modify page blocks when the integration has access.

faq.question: Can it permanently delete Notion content?
faq.answer: The documented Notion actions archive pages or blocks. Users should still review archive actions carefully.

faq.question: Why must the assistant search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching tools first helps use the current parameters.

faq.question: What happens if a database is not found?
faq.answer: The database may not be shared with the integration, or the search query may not match the title.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive workspaces?
faq.answer: Use it only with trusted Rube and Notion connections, because it can read and modify workspace data.
